pull.title: kernel: Chainparams and headerssync updates pre-31.0
pull.body:
Update chainparams and headerssync params per the release process.

Also added new assumeutxo snapshots for each network. I've uploaded snapshots to https://achow101.com/files/utxo-snapshots/

events.message: kernel: update assumed blockchain and chainstate sizes

rename.to: kernel: Chainparams and headerssync updates pre-31.0

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [janb84](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34677#pullrequestreview-3860908095), [Sjors](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34677#issuecomment-3992684188), [sipa](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34677#pullrequestreview-3897259542) | | Concept ACK | [jaonoctus](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34677#pullrequestreview-3869225696) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#26201](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/26201) (Remove Taproot BIP 9 deployment by Sjors) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first. ### LLM Linter (✨ experimental) Possible places where named args for integral literals may be used (e.g. `func(x, /*named_arg=*/0)` in C++, and `func(x, named_arg=0)` in Python): - [datetime(2028, 10, 10)] in contrib/devtools/headerssync-params.py 2026-02-26 07:23:28

events.message: kernel: Add recent assumeutxo snapshot info

events.body:
ACK 44538f8ada868cb73ffa7a4bfb5267046d3f6b85

Checked some hashes with block heights /chainwork / getchaintxstats, all match (for mainnet. testnets checked only public information) 

Mainnet: 
https://mempool.space/block/00000000000000000000ccebd6d74d9194d8dcdc1d177c478e094bfad51ba5ac => 938'343 

MinimumChainwork matches:

<details>

```sh
bitcoin-cli getblockheader 00000000000000000000ccebd6d74d9194d8dcdc1d177c478e094bfad51ba5ac
{
  "hash": "00000000000000000000ccebd6d74d9194d8dcdc1d177c478e094bfad51ba5ac",
  "height": 938343,
[...]
  **"chainwork": "0000000000000000000000000000000000000001128750f82f4c366153a3a030"**,
}
```

</details>

https://mempool.space/block/0000000000000000000147034958af1652b2b91bba607beacc5e72a56f0fb5ee = > 935'000

getchaintxstats match ; 
<details>


</details>

https://mempool.space/signet/block/0000000577f2741bb30cd9d39d6d71b023afbeb9764f6260786a97969d5c9ac0 => 290'000

comments.body:
`m_chain_tx_count` seems to be wrong. Is this a typo?

```diff
-.m_chain_tx_count = 14141057,
+.m_chain_tx_count = 13870119,
```

---

`$ bitcoin-cli gettxoutsetinfo hash_serialized_3`

```json
{
  "height": 120000,
  "bestblock": "000000000bd2317e51b3c5794981c35ba894ce27d3e772d5c39ecd9cbce01dc8",
  "txouts": 13870119,
  "bogosize": 1142705000,
  "hash_serialized_3": "10b05d05ad468d0971162e1b222a4aa66caca89da2bb2a93f8f37fb29c4794b0",
  "total_amount": 5999799.97251533,
  "transactions": 9181935,
  "disk_size": 1023009172
}
```
No, it is correct. It's the count of transactions in the chain, not the number of UTXOs.

@achow101 Oh I see, it should match `txcount` from `getchaintxstats` instead. Pardon

In that case, it seems to be right then.

---

`$ bitcoin-cli getchaintxstats 4096`

```json
{
  "time": 1769234583,
  "txcount": 14141057,
  "window_final_block_hash": "000000000bd2317e51b3c5794981c35ba894ce27d3e772d5c39ecd9cbce01dc8",
  "window_final_block_height": 120000,
  "window_block_count": 4096,
  "window_interval": 2168096,
  "window_tx_count": 56313,
  "txrate": 0.02597348087907547
}
```
